skill.name: incident-response-incident-response
skill.description: Orchestrate multi-agent incident response workflows using modern SRE practices for rapid incident resolution and blameless postmortems.
skill.summary: Multi-agent incident command system for rapid detection, investigation, resolution, and postmortem documentation.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 11 static findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate incident response workflow guide (markdown documentation). The 'external_commands' detection refers to markdown backticks for file paths, not shell execution. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' and 'system/network reconnaissance' detections are scanner misinterpretations of incident response terminology (severity levels, observability analysis, root cause analysis). No actual security risks present.

high_findings.title: External Commands Detection (False Positive)
high_findings.description: Scanner detected 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at SKILL.md:23 - this is markdown formatting (backticks around file path 'resources/implementation-playbook.md'), not shell execution.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks are clearly markdown inline code syntax, not shell command execution. No shell commands or Ruby code exist in this skill.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detection (False Positive)
high_findings.description: Scanner detected 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at multiple lines - these are false positives. The skill contains no cryptographic code.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: No cryptographic algorithms are present in this markdown documentation skill. The scanner is incorrectly flagging random words.

high_findings.title: System/Network Reconnaissance Detection (False Positive)
high_findings.description: Scanner detected 'system/network reconnaissance' at multiple lines - these are false positives. The terms refer to legitimate incident response activities (severity classification, observability analysis, root cause analysis).
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: These terms appear in the context of incident response workflow phases (detection, triage, investigation) - standard SRE terminology, not malicious reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: This skill provides a structured multi-phase incident response workflow for AI agents, enabling rapid detection, investigation, resolution, and postmortem documentation following modern SRE principles.
actual_capabilities: Coordinate multi-agent incident response across detection, investigation, resolution, and communication phases
Classify incident severity (P0-P3) and determine appropriate response protocols
Orchestrate observability analysis using distributed tracing, metrics, and log aggregation
Guide root cause analysis using Five Whys methodology and system debugging
Generate blameless postmortem documentation with action items and process improvements
Implement monitoring enhancements and system hardening to prevent incident recurrence
limitations: This is a workflow guide only - it does not execute actual system operations
Requires other specialized skills for specific tasks (deployment, security scanning, debugging)
Does not have direct access to monitoring systems or incident management tools
Relies on Claude/Codex agent orchestration, not direct infrastructure access

use_cases.title: SRE Team Lead managing production outage
use_cases.description: Use the full workflow to coordinate team response, maintain incident command structure, and ensure proper communication during a sev-1 incident.

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer conducting post-incident review
use_cases.description: Use Phase 5 (Postmortem & Prevention) to document incident timeline, identify root causes, and create action items for monitoring improvements.

use_cases.title: On-call engineer performing initial triage
use_cases.description: Use Phase 1 (Detection & Triage) to quickly classify incident severity, assess impact, and determine initial mitigation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Initial Incident Triage
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the incident-response skill to triage this alert: [DESCRIBE ALERT]. Determine severity level (P0-P3), identify affected services, assess user impact, and recommend initial mitigation actions.
prompt_templates.scenario: When receiving an alert from PagerDuty, Opsgenie, or monitoring system

prompt_templates.title: Deep Investigation Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the incident-response skill to investigate this incident: [INCIDENT DESCRIPTION]. Conduct deep debugging, security assessment, and performance analysis to identify root cause.
prompt_templates.scenario: When initial triage is complete and detailed investigation is needed

prompt_templates.title: Emergency Deployment Coordination
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the incident-response skill to coordinate this emergency fix: [INCIDENT AND FIX DESCRIPTION]. Execute deployment with validation, monitoring, and rollback readiness.
prompt_templates.scenario: When a fix is ready to be deployed to production

prompt_templates.title: Postmortem Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the incident-response skill to conduct a blameless postmortem for: [INCIDENT SUMMARY]. Document timeline, root cause, what went well, what could improve, and create action items.

output_examples.input: Use incident-response skill to triage: API service returning 500 errors for 30% of requests
output_examples.output:
Severity: P1/SEV-2 (Major degradation)

Affected Services: API Gateway, User Service
User Impact: 30% failed requests, primarily authenticated users

Initial Mitigation:
1. Enable circuit breaker for User Service
2. Check for recent deployments to roll back
3. Scale up API Gateway capacity

Incident Commander: [Assign]
Technical Lead: [Assign]
Communications Lead: [Assign]

output_examples.input: Use incident-response skill to create postmortem for yesterday's database outage
output_examples.output:
## Blameless Postmortem - Database Outage

### Timeline
- 14:00 - Alert fired: Database CPU at 99%
- 14:05 - Incident declared P1
- 14:15 - Rollback attempted
- 14:30 - Root cause: Connection pool exhaustion
- 14:45 - Fix deployed
- 15:00 - Incident resolved

### Root Cause
Migration script created 10x normal connections

### What Went Well
- Fast detection (2 min)
- Clear communication

### Action Items
1. Add connection pool monitoring - Owner: Jane - Due: Feb 28
2. Update runbook for migrations - Owner: Bob - Due: Mar 1

best_practices: Assign clear incident commander and roles within the first 5 minutes of any P0/P1 incident
Update stakeholder communication every 15-30 minutes during active incidents
Complete blameless postmortem within 48 hours with specific, assignable action items
anti_patterns: Skipping severity classification and jumping straight to debugging without understanding impact
Blaming individuals in postmortems rather than focusing on system improvements
Delaying communication to stakeholders until full resolution is achieved

faq.question: Does this skill execute actual incident response actions?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides workflow guidance and orchestrates other specialized skills. It does not directly access monitoring systems, execute deployments, or modify infrastructure.

faq.question: Can this skill replace my incident management platform?
faq.answer: No. This skill is designed to work with existing tools like PagerDuty, Opsgenie, and status pages. It provides the workflow logic but relies on external systems for alerting and communication.

faq.question: What severity levels does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports P0 (SEV-1) through P3 (SEV-4) severity levels. P0/P1 require immediate all-hands response, while P2/P3 follow standard response procedures.

faq.question: How does this skill handle security incidents?
faq.answer: Phase 2 includes a Security Assessment step that checks for DDoS indicators, authentication failures, data exposure risks, and suspicious access patterns.

faq.question: Can junior engineers use this skill effectively?
faq.answer: Yes. The structured workflow guides less experienced team members through incident response phases with clear prompts and expected outputs for each step.

faq.question: What makes this skill different from general debugging guides?
faq.answer: This skill provides a comprehensive incident command system (ICS) with multi-agent orchestration, focusing on coordination, communication, and prevention rather than just technical troubleshooting.
